name: Sea Island Creole English

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Sea Island Creole English (gull1241).

notes: Skeleton. English-lexifier creole of the South Carolina and Georgia Sea Islands, written in a Latin, largely English-based spelling. Grapheme map uses English digraph conventions (ch, sh, ng; oo [u], ee [i]) but reflects the creole's own phonology where it diverges, notably th realised as a stop [t]/[d]. Gullah has no fixed standard orthography and much text is eye-dialect respelling of English; unstressed-vowel reduction and any prosodic detail are not recoverable and are left unencoded, so a real PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Sea Island Creole English (gull1241)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
